sections.heading: Follow-Up
sections.content: For patients with localized disease in remission, follow-up frequency is determined by clinical need, consisting of history and physical examination, with diagnostic tests for symptomatic patients. For patients with no evidence of clinical disease, clinical follow-up with imaging every 6–12 months for up to 5 years is warranted.12 To monitor for disease recurrence, detect late effects of treatment, and manage survivorship issues. The primary goal is symptom control and quality of life in incurable disease.12 Screening for treatment-related toxicities (e.g., neuropathy, renal toxicity from platinum, cardiac toxicity from anthracyclines) based on prior therapies. Monitoring for secondary malignancies related to radiation or chemotherapy. Patterns vary by tumor type and primary site. A latent primary cancer may emerge during the natural course, though uncommon. In 20% to 50% of patients, the primary tumor is not identified even after postmortem examination.8,15,16
